rounds: Optional short recruiter/HR call - resume and project questions, no coding
Technical Round 1 - 45 min, coding on shared Google Doc, easy-medium difficulty
Technical Round 2 - 45 min, coding on shared Google Doc, medium-hard difficulty
Google STEP.difficulty: Easy to Medium, sometimes Medium-Hard in round 2
Google STEP.format_notes: No IDE - code is written on a Google Doc. Interviewers expect you to think out loud, optimize a brute-force solution, and discuss edge cases and time/space complexity.
Google STEP.behavioral_focus: Minimal - light questions about projects/resume, no formal system design
resume_flags_positive: Competitive programming experience (Codeforces, CodeChef, Kick Start, Code Jam)

rounds: Behavioral/Recruiter Round - fit and communication questions
Technical Round 2 (sometimes) - 45 min, slightly deeper coding
Microsoft Explore.difficulty: Easier than STEP - explicitly designed for students new to software engineering
Microsoft Explore.format_notes: Less DSA-intense than STEP. Some rounds include reasoning/behavioral questions (e.g., explaining a technical concept simply, basic language comparisons like C vs Java).
Microsoft Explore.behavioral_focus: Moderate - communication and reasoning matter alongside code correctness
resume_flags_positive: GitHub or portfolio showing practical work
Clear, well-structured resume with no broken links
